Tunnel 1 conveyor is tracking to the west causing caramel to deposit off the cookie bed.
I will be there when I can, until then, would you be able to check the rollers to make sure they're correctly positioned? Just to eliminate one possible cause

We found two major issues with Tunnel 1. There is a roller missing underneath the chocolate depositor, which is causing the beltnto drag against the equipment underneath. And one of the drive rollers wasn't installed correctly creating potential for significant belt damage. The team is working on correcting these issues while the line is down. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Thank you guys
Tunnel 1 is fixed and ready. Maintenance is still working on MM5, and we have 27 bins of accumulation remaining. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Matrix 5 is ready, but i think it's having the undersized funnel issue like 3 and 4
It could be the cones or the foot print of the product is to big, I’m having the front end adjust it to see if it helps
Do we have a spec size for them?

We are starting Tunnel 2 now. Caleb York, Dakota Adair, we've recognized that the pieces were too big at first, so one of the first adjustments we made was to reduce the piece-size to be as close to the minimum as possible. We've also adjusted the decorator setting to allow for maximum chocolate coverage which will prevent the caramel from sticking. We still have ~15 bins of accumulation, but I've instructed the team to mix that product with the new product that has the changes to make it smaller. Curt Willis
We are scrapping two containers of accumulated product that appear to be severely deteriorated.Charity Young, Angelica Perez
We've paused the line due to the Tunnel 1 belt folding over on itself under the shaker, and we've called Caleb York to assist with the belt tracking issue. Dakota Adair Curt Willis

At the start of the line, the belt is observed drifting to one side; this causes only a drop of caramel to emerge at the other end, and upon reaching the inclusion point, the pieces stick together. I suggest assigning someone to remove the stuck pieces to prevent further issues—such as jams in the molds or cones—and the line has been stopped.
Is there a way to center the band or remove that injector? William Beattie Conner Boyd
some pieces are larger and are getting stuck in the cones.
It is possible if they are not removed.
The problem is that the belt is shifting to one side; I'll wait to be informed about the steps to take and let you know.

Sollich update 3:
Matrix 3; all the open bags
Matrix 4 All bags are rejected based on weight.

We've adjusted the Tunnel 1 belt. We found another roller that wasn't installed correctly. Then line is running now. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
We need a pause; the sealing on M3 hasn't improved—bags are still coming out open and poorly sealed.William Beattie, Angelica Perez

*and center it. 
 
There was a photo-eye that was slightly bent in, and the tracking roller bolt was too far in, not allowing enough movement in the piston.
Have they worked on improving it?
The operators have made adjustments, but they have not been favorable.
If we need to put something on hold because it's open pouches, please do so. Employees need to know that we can't bring open bags to the final pallet. Alfonsina Gil William Beattie
After lunch, I will look into all 3 Matrix machines again. Do we have the CL for the TJ brookies? I think the bag size was similar

The Tunnel 1 belt is still tracking West on the bottom, and is close to folding over on itself again. We will follow the advice of Caleb York and pause in order to loosen the tension





 and move the belt manually. Dakota Adair Curt Willis

It has been observed that, while the product itself looks very good, the bags are splitting at the bottom due to poor sealing; this issue is recurring, leading to further product deterioration. Two additional containers will be scrapped, and the product will continue to be monitored to determine if further scrapping is necessary.

We adjusted the Tunnel 1 belt, but haven't been able to run long enough to verify if it's fixed or not. All 3 matrixes are having problems. MM3 and MM5 aren't sealing many of the bags, adding to our pouch scrap, and the hands for MM4 became loose and were catching on the suction cups. Caleb York and Dakota Adair are here now helping investigate the issues. Curt Willis
Four containers of damaged product are being scrapped.

I have taken two bags from each batch to conduct a visual inspection of the product and continue to find it in a very poor state; I am not at all satisfied with its appearance, and I will inform the supervisor and manager.
At first glance, it may appear to have an off-spec look. I will investigate what is happening and talk to Dakota Adair Let's see what solutions we can come up with to improve it. Charity Young

I can confirm that what Alfonsina has been reporting is true; the final product looks good, but when it is passed twice due to the matrices, the quality of the appearance is lost. 


 


We need to make sure we don't have poorly sealed or open bags so that the product doesn't have to be reworked and processed twice. 


 

















Unacceptable product.


William Beattie Dakota Adair
Caleb York and myself with continue looking into a solution for the pouch and product issues

We have 3 pallets produced so far and we're putting it on hold. I'm not according with the appearance; my concern is the caramel as it looks.

We will perform another viscosity and water activity test.

Another issue is the pieces become stuck together when we use the deeper bins. I've instructed the team to scrap any accumulation in the deeper bins, and to only use the shallower bins from now on. Dakota Adair Curt Willis Angelica Perez Alfonsina Gil

3.9g (pecans)
MAX: 11.1g- 8g (caramel) =
3.1g (pecans)

Timeline:
At approximately 3:20am, the bolt securing the arm on MM4 fell off, prompting the operator to immediately stop the line and begin searching for the missing part. The collection bin beneath the machine was moved to inspect its contents; however, the bin had already been emptied on the mezzanine by a member of the production team. Maintenance was unable to locate the missing bolt.

At 4:20 a.m., all product remaining in the matrix was emptied, inspected and discarded. In addition, all finished product produced on MM4 was placed on hold and re-screened through the X-ray machine to verify that the missing part was not present in any of the pouches.

At 4:35 a.m., QA found a bolt in the area that appeared similar to the missing one; however, maintenance confirmed it was not the correct piece. The recovered bolt was tested through the X-ray machine five times. It was rejected on three passes and was not rejected on two passes.

At 4:50 a.m., the team began re-screening all affected pouches through the X-ray machine.

At 5:20 a.m., maintenance located a replacement bolt and installed it on the equipment. However, the original missing part was still not recovered.

The pallet for Matrix 4 has finished going through the X-ray scanner again.
Jody Howse Ebrima Dibba Charity Young

Buffer tank temp is steady. Just in case, I made sure PTL temps are in range and the decrystalizer is functioning as it should.

Its less likely but possible the sensor is not working as it should or the read out screen (little digital meter on the electrical box) is not working well.
 
Update, I was told that the temp readout on the bottom was also showing low. More likely it is a water level issue if it continues to happen. 10 minutes out here and it hasn't dropped.

We are going to make one change to the tempering unit that feeds the enrober / bottomer. 

Here is the picture I took 1 hours ago and we are focused on the blue. 


Setpoint = 45.0C and Actual is 45.1C

We want to do a 2 phases approach. 

Phase 1: change setpoint from 45.0C to 39.0C

Take two tempers over 1 hour. It should actually stabilize. 

Phase 2: change setpoint from 39.0C to 33.0C. 

Continue to take tempers.
This is a picture of what we ran on milk in L1 before the move.
Is the pipe feeding the enrober physically warm to the touch? Tempered chocolate (feeding the enrober) should feel slightly cold.

The Truffle line has operated during the second shift without issues. Tempering has remained within established parameters, and the screen has shown no deviations or instances of foreign material.


Regarding weights, the pre-piece ranges from 8.8 to 9.0 g, while the final piece varies between 14.1 and 14.8 g, meeting specifications. From an organoleptic standpoint, the pieces exhibit acceptable characteristics, with good texture and flavor.


In the packaging area, the flow wraps appear to be in good condition, with no issues requiring team notification. Likewise, box weights comply with established standards, and the information printed on the wrappers matches that on the final labels.


Personnel are adhering to GMPs, and overall, operations are proceeding stably in compliance with established requirements and conditions.
